etymology.description: phonetic 豆 (dòu) + semantic 頁 (head) -> head
etymology.interpretation: The character 頭 combines the component 豆 (dòu, 'bean'), which provides the sound, with the component 頁 (yè, 'head'), which provides the meaning of 'head'. As such, 豆 imparts the pronunciation, while 頁 depicts the relevant concept of 'head'. This combination effectively conveys the meaning as 'head' or 'top'.
etymology.simplification: Simplified from 頭 by reducing the phonetic 豆 to ⺀, and the semantic 頁 was modified to 大.

examples.chinese: 他的头发很短(他的頭髮很短)。
他们站在山顶上(他們站在山頂上)，欣赏美景(欣賞美景)。
examples.pinyin: Tāmen zhàn zài shāndǐng shàng, xīnshǎng měijǐng.
examples.english: They stood on the mountain top, enjoying the beautiful view.

examples.chinese: 有一个苹果掉在了她的头上(有一個蘋果掉在了她的頭上)。
examples.pinyin: Yǒu yī gè píngguǒ diào zài le tā de tóu shàng.
examples.english: An apple fell on her head.
